description: Multisig commands — set, delete, and list multi-signature signer lists on XRPL accounts

userStories.title: multisig set command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to configure a multi-signature signer list on my account from the CLI so I can require multiple parties to authorise transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Read SignerListSet docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/signerlistset.md before implementing
Create src/commands/multisig.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts

xrpl multisig set subcommand with: --quorum n (required, SignerQuorum > 0), --signer address:weight (repeatable, at least 1 required); help text example: --signer rAlice...XYZ:3 --signer rBob...ABC:2 --quorum 4
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if no --signer is provided
CLI exits with error if --quorum exceeds sum of all signer weights
CLI exits with error if --quorum is 0 (suggest using multisig delete instead)
CLI exits with error if more than 32 signers are provided
CLI exits with error if a signer address appears more than once
CLI exits with error if address:weight format is invalid (missing colon, non-integer weight, weight <= 0)
Parse --signer by splitting on last colon; validate address with isValidAddress() from xrpl.js
tests/e2e/multisig/multisig.validation.test.ts: no signers, quorum > sum of weights, quorum=0, >32 signers, duplicate address, invalid format (no network)
tests/e2e/multisig/multisig.set.test.ts: set a 2-of-3 signer list and verify via multisig list; update existing list; --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: multisig delete and multisig list commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to delete a signer list and query current signers to manage multi-sig lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: xrpl multisig delete subcommand: sends SignerListSet with SignerQuorum=0 and no SignerEntries field; standard key material and output options
xrpl multisig list <address> subcommand: uses account_objects RPC with type 'signer_list'
multisig list default output: quorum value on first line, then each signer as 'address (weight: n)'
multisig list shows 'No signer list configured.' if account has no signer list
--json outputs raw JSON
--node supported
tests/e2e/multisig/multisig.delete.test.ts: set then delete signer list, verify multisig list shows no signer list after deletion; --json, --dry-run
tests/e2e/multisig/multisig.list.test.ts: set a signer list, verify multisig list shows correct quorum and signers with correct weights; --json
Typecheck passes
